item.title: Secrets audit trail — append-only JSONL with HMAC chain
steps: Create src/security/secrets/audit.ts with SecretAuditEvent type matching PRODUCT.md data model (seq, event_type, secret_name, timestamp, prev_hmac, hmac)
Implement append-only JSONL writer: each line is a JSON SecretAuditEvent, file permissions 600
Implement HMAC chain: each event's hmac = HMAC-SHA256(key, JSON(event_without_hmac) + prev_hmac), first event uses empty prev_hmac
Implement chain verification: read all events, recompute HMAC chain, report any tampering
Add `secrets audit` sub-subcommand to src/cli/secrets.ts: display chronological event log with formatted table (seq, timestamp, event_type, secret_name)
Add `--verify` flag to `secrets audit` that validates HMAC chain integrity
Integrate audit event emission into FEAT-044 add/list and FEAT-045 rotate/revoke flows (export appendAuditEvent function)
Emit accessed-by-deploy events from the deploy pipeline (hook into src/docker/ deploy flow)
Write unit tests for HMAC chain computation, verification, and tamper detection
acceptanceCriteria: `clawhq secrets audit` displays chronological log of all secret events
Audit trail is append-only JSONL file with 600 permissions
Each event has HMAC linking to previous event (tamper-evident chain)
`clawhq secrets audit --verify` validates chain integrity and reports tampering
Events are recorded for: add, rotate, revoke, and deploy-access operations
